[IMAGE:0]It's getting on for midnight, so we can proceed with a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism and I'll do my best to answer it. Any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. (Any question received after then will not get an answer, and will more than likely just be deleted.) If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.0 of The Magic Monday FAQ here.

The photo? One of my readers mentioned a little while back that a namesake of mine was a R&B saxophonist back in the 1940s and 1950s. I decided to check him out, and here he is in all his musical glory. Check out one of his tunes here. As far as I know, he's not a relative, though you never know -- there's been far more interracial marriage in American history, to say nothing of less formal interactions, than anybody likes to admit in these polarized times. If he does turn out to be a distant cousin I won't be unhappy. 

(If Big John Greer was an occultist, that fact didn't make it into his online bios, but of course it's always possible -- between hoodoo on one end of the African-American community, and a plethora of urban magical lodges on the other end, there was no shortage of black American occultism when his career was rolling...) 

[IMAGE:1]

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use the button above to access my online tip jar. If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here. 
 
[IMAGE:2]I've also had quite a few people over the years ask me where they should buy my books, and here's the answer. Bookshop.org is an alternative online bookstore that supports local bookstores and authors, which a certain other gargantuan corporation doesn't, and I now have a shop there, which you can check out here. Please consider patronizing it if you'd like to purchase any of my books online.

And don't forget to look up your Pangalactic New Age Soul Signature at CosmicOom.com.

With that said, have at it! 

***This Magic Monday is now closed. See you next week!***

Good evening!

Thanks very much for the time and effort you put into these posts, and for cultivating such an interesting and pleasant community.

I've got a bit of a grab-bag of questions today, on which I equally welcome the commentariat's input:

1. I know they are not a part of your religious practice, but do you have any thoughts on what kind of beings the elves of Germanic myth are or might be?

2. This may be a question for the Lords of Karma, but here goes: presumably, if a tragedy affects a group of folks, like the loss of a beloved family member, then somehow the karma of all of those affected led them to that experience one way or another, and that is part of why they were incarnated into that place and time. If that is correct, how might one person's karmic culmination interact with this broader group? More concretely, is it self-centered to wonder if karmic culmination due to my beginning a spiritual practice was part of the explanation for a death in my family that hurt others just as much as me?

3. To what degree (if at all), does magical symbolic (e.g. tarot, ogham, cabala) or mythological thinking enter into how you approach secular topics (e.g. economics, politics)? For example, do you ever look at a political situation and think something like "ah yes, I'm seeing some reversed Nuin here that will likely lead to some reversed Mor" and thereby gain insight that more traditional methods of analysis might not bring?

4. As a bit of a flip to the above: your writing has a fair number of examples of applying esoteric lessons to exoteric problems (like, say, ternaries resolving an inadequate binary). Do you have any favorite examples of a mundane insight or way of thinking granting a spiritual or magical insight?

5. Have you heard of "The Twelve Type Enneagram", and if so, do you have any thoughts on it? If not, it's a personality typing system that describes folks by their primary planetary energy, and I was wondering if the descriptions it gives might be good ways to better get to know those energies.

Thanks much,
Jeff

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) When I meet some, I'll certainly let you know. ;-) Seriously, I don't happen to know -- there are a lot of different spiritual beings around, and they don't necessarily fit into anyone's categories.

2) Keep in mind that everyone dies sooner or later, and having someone in your life die is thus one of the most inevitable of all human experiences. If that's karma, it's everyone's karma. The tangled way that karma works in the lives of groups of people is difficult to trace, and especially so with karmic culmination -- you might have simply had that coming at that time anyway.

3) That's not really the way I think. My magic and my politics are both shaped by a broader philosophical context, and that's what tends to show up when I think about politics: "Okay, I was wondering when the enantiodroma was going to cut in," and that sort of thing.

4) Nothing particularly comes to mind.

5) No, I hadn't heard of it. It's not the kind of thing I would hear about, since I don't keep track of current pop occulture.

comments.title: Energetic interference with cell salts?
comments.content:
Greetings Uncle John! I have a question about proper use and storage of cell salts. Cell salts are a subset of homeopathy, are they not? My wife has begun taking homeopathy workshops, and one thing she was taught there was to be careful with how to handle remedies: do not store them right next to electrical appliances, and do not handle them directly with your hands as the hands are our main point of contact with all of the who-knows-what there is in the world that you don't want messing with the subtle levels of your remedies. Instead, put pills in a cap or spoon, and from there deliver them to your mouth.

1. Does this make sense to you, and have you ever worried about this? As for me, I was storing my cell salts in my bathroom medicine cabinet... which has both a power outlet installed in it directly next to where I kept the salts, and an electrified anti-steaming heated mirror that automatically goes on when the lights are on, directly in front of where I kept them. I didn't actually know it worked by electrical heating until recently-- the temperature issue might also interfere with the potency of the salts. Do you think there's a good chance I might have hurt my batch?

2. I have an acquaintance who has bought a bunch of 3x salts with the intention of transforming them into a larger supply of 6x salts. In the process of succussion and trituration and so forth, do you know if there is there normally any kind of prophylaxis used to avoid touching the ingredients directly?

replies.title: Re: Energetic interference with cell salts?
replies.content:
1) Nope. Homeopathic medicines vary in their vulnerability to electric fields and the like, and also in their reaction to bodily contact. The more raw material there is in the medicine, the less vulnerable it is, and mineral-derived homeopathics are also more robust than those derived from plants (which are themselves more robust than animal-sourced medicines). On both counts, cell salts are very sturdy; they'll put up with almost anything and still keep their potency, unlike some others.

2) Yes. They shouldn't be touched by hand and the instruments used to handle them should be cleaned, rinsed with cold water, and dried thoroughly before and after use.

replies.title: Re: Energetic interference with cell salts?
replies.content:
Agreed on every account.

Also, considering that homeopathy works on the etheric/subtle level of being, the frequency/complexity of the electromagnetic field should have a bigger effect than the raw power behind it. For this reason, power cords should be Ok as long as you keep your meds one or two inches away. On the other hand, electronics cast off noxious fields that could break or at least twist the signature of the remedy (nobody has done the research, AFAIK). This is specially true of cell phones, Wifi routers and other radio devices because those have transceivers and antennas designed to project an actual signal into the distance (though purely passive receivers are probably not that bad).

If you think about it, the fact that homeopathy has any effect *at all* in this time and age is a Miracle!!!

replies.title: Re: Energetic interference with cell salts?
replies.content:
I can vouch for the phone signal and in general some electronics, but not all, to me it makes the room feel "on-edge" impossible to settle in as if they emit some sort of flash that saturates my psychic senses. Ever since I banned my phone from being close to my room I can sleep much more peacefully.

Has anyone found ways to deal with sensitivity to that kind of stuff on everyday life? I am embarrassed to say that I looked if someone had tried a faraday cage (a set of metal wire mesh that disrupts electromagnetic radiation) and it turns out there is a beanie with one embedded on it, but I felt like a tin foil hat maniac and didn't look further into it but I guess there might actually be something to that...

This problem seems restricted to a small set of themes, and I've been practicing for years without encountering this phenomena.

1) I'm not looking for health advice, but I was wondering if you've heard stories or rumors about this system coming with health consequences? It feels more like an initiation than a problem, but I was wondering if you knew of any old lore or crackpot-pseudoscience relevant to this?

2) Seeing the incredible depth of the DMH, how far should I take it? A year of practice? A decade? Is there some specific attainment I'm looking for or is it obvious when I reach it?

replies.content:
Yes, indeed! The answer to the unspoken question is, of course, that the bulk of the Russian émigré community came from the privileged classes and social strata of Tsarist Russia.

For a number of years in High School and College I dated a schoolmate who came from that community. One year I went with her and some of her Russian friends to the grand Masquerade Ball that the Russian émigré community held in San Francisco every year. The first prize for men was won that year by a very elderly Russian who wore his old uniform from his days as a cadet in the foremost military academy of Tsarist Russia. My date commented, every year one of those old military uniforms wins the first prize.

At that time (late 1950s - early 1960s) there was a Russian-language organization for boys and young men equivalent to the Boy Scouts (but quite independent of American Scouting), who were called Razvedchiki. Every year, my friend told me on another occasion, there was a special Panikhida (funeral service) for the Scouts who had died the year before. "Were there so many?" I asked. She replied, "Oh yes, some of the older Scouts are smuggled into Russia every year to sabotage the Communist regime. Most of them are probably soon caught and executed, because we stop hearing from them. But there are always lots of other young men eager to take their places." It was an open secret among the émigrés that the Razvedchiki cooperated heavily with the US intelligence services (at that time, chiefly with the CIA). But even without CIA involvement, there would have been plenty of young men eager to do and die in order to restore the last Tsar's heir to the Russian throne. I don't quite remember the name of that heir after so many years, but if I am not mistaken, he had been living in Paris. All my émigré friends knew who he was, and some households even displayed his picture,

comments.title: CosDoc questions. Planets and swarms.
comments.content:
Hello Mr. Greer.

I'm slowly progressing with CosDoc (painfully slowly, but as you said, there's no need to rush).

Currently I'm trying to make sense of the planets and swarms. Reading your posts on the issue clarifies some things, but obviously it doesn't make the work for us. One thing I try to remember is that the text is a metaphore for a frame of complex system behaviours.
So, the first reading leads to the Sun God, and the planets as Entities that condition us mortal beings. This level is what all the commentaries in the post seem to relate.

My second reading put me at the level of a social group, where the Logos is not a person, but the 'spirit' of the group. Here, individual sparks are the people belonging to the group, and every planet is the 'sphere of matter' in which they interact. In this second reading, Lords of Flame are scientist and mystics that discover the laws of Nature by experimentation (free to roam outside), Lords of Form are academics and teologists that compile the knowledge in useful texts for others to study (following the paths carved by others), and Lords of Mind are teachers and masters that explain the topics to the following stundents (individualized). There are many weak links, but makes some sense. Now, when I think of the Lords of Flame I think of gurus that learned some Truth of Life (TM), then the Lords of Form are the administrators of the Lodge that follows the teachings of the gurus and are in charge of the books, and then the Lords of Mind are the students and future teachers in this institution. Here, every planet is a degree, so being in 3rd grade is the same as being in Mercury classroom. Students in 6th grade will have the honor to be accepted in conferences given by the gurus, when they are back to the institution.

The third reading put myself in the role of the Logos of the universe I create around me. Here is where I'm struggling. I'll try to recapitulate my understanding of these chapters:
Since I cannot see me directly for who I am, I probe a myriad of movements, slowly learning what is possible in the physical realm, the most accesible to me. What troubles me here is that Fortune says each of this movement is a divine spark and thus has the potential to become a Logos itself. I can't picture a random movement of my feet as a potential Logos.
Once I know subconsciously what the physical body is, after a time of introspection, I can start experimenting with the ethereal body.
Then they come the concepts, I give names and labels to the experimented reactions, so now I know what are my skin, my feet, my eyes, but also what is outside. The key about concepts is that we need to go back to concepts of the earlier planes to give labels to the subtler planes. That's why we say Libido is a Force: it is not a Force in the physical sense, but the effect in the ethereal plane is similar to what a physical force provokes in the physical plane: it pushes something (me) towards somewhere (having sex). Here, Fortune explains that in order to conceptualize effects happening in a given plane, we need to retrace concepts from the physical plane, find an equivalent in the following plane and so on. So, concepts such as force and movement mean a different thing in each plane. A movement in the astral is so different from a movement in the ethereal as this is different from the physical.
The processes exposed above are subconscious, they are happening without a direct intention of our mind.

The Lord of Mind are then our conscious thoughts, the voice we hear when we are thinking. They work with the concepts that the subconscious mind has created and learn by vibration (repetition). These thoughts are able to interact with the thoughts that will follow them individually, and this way we can follow a line of thought.

We need a time of introspection then, for integrating all those experiences, concepts and thoughts in ourselves. It is only after we have internalized the concepts and the thoughts of one plane that we can start interacting with the following one.

This is what builds subtle bodies around us. We need to watch, interact, conceptualize and become habituated to the next plane to be able to form a body of that plane, until all bodies have been created. Forming a body of the 1st plane matter starts a different process. Introspection is no longer needed, evolution proceeds by experimenting again in the lower planes with the knowledge acquired in the subtler ones. When this happens, we can experience conscious dreams (Lords of Mind meeting Lords of Flame in the 6th planet).

I hope I am not getting this awfully wrong.

comments.content:
Dear JMG,

I've been comparing what we've been learning from Doctrine and Ritual with concepts in my DMH studies. Both strands cast a lot of light on each other, but sometimes I got caught up in the different names each system has for what may be similar concepts. A lot of seemingly worthwhile material is coming out of meditations and reflections on both systems, but I worry that in doing this, I may be forcing concepts on a Procrustean bed.

So with the assumption that these two systems are not identical, but have similar concepts in parts, could I ask a few questions?

1. Is the circle of Ceugant the rough equivalent to Kether?*
2. If so, that could mean that Ain Soph is the equivalent of Annwn, in other words the stuff (unsure of the name) inside Ceridwen's cauldron?

* I ask, because a series of insights seems to work. I got a clue from the theory chapter in the DMH where the Tree of Life is compared to the Wheel of Life. There is a passage which says that "the ninth [Sphere of the Tree of Life] is the realm of the stars, and the tenth represents the divine realm beyond the stars."

I had assumed that Kether, meaning 'crown' in Hebrew as per Levi, corresponded to the Station of Alban Arthuan, which also has the symbol of the crown. But it also corresponds to 'a place in the northern stars' as one of the Inner Places of the Stations used in Ogham path working.

This led me, in short, to tying together a few concepts. It's not yet fully fleshed out, and there are hole in it, as you'll see. If Kether, which would represent the divine realm, is equivalent to Ceugant, then Alban Arthuan could correspond to the ninth Sphere on the Tree. In retrospect, it doesn't totally work, because it appears that the planetary correspondences of the Spheres seem to differ depending on where you look.

But despite this, it could still lead to some correspondences to Doctrine and Ritual if Ceugant corresponds to Kether, the unity. This makes more sense rather than Alban Arthuan corresponding to Kether, because both systems indicate that what is represented by the words Kether or Ceugant are inaccessible and unknowable by humans, whereas Alban Arthuan is an accessible state of consciousness.

After the unity, it divides into the binary. Whereas in Levi this corresponds to Chokmah, perhaps the first binary we get in the DMH system is that of Celi and Ced, the male and female principles, and also perhaps the equivalent in the DMH system to the axiom 'as above, so below'. These correspond also to the solar and telluric currents, and Spirit Above and Spirit Below in the SOP.

The idea in Levi that the sun impregnates the earth, resulting in the creation of the earth's 'subtle body', or the great agent, seems to reference this.

If that is the case, then the 8 Stations of the Wheel could be like filters which colour the emanations of the divine realm, themselves filtered through their manifestations as the Sun and the centre of the Earth and personified by Celi and Ced.

I still need to think a lot more on all this, but I thought I would run it by you to shed light on why I'm asking about these correspondences.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Trying to work out exact correspondences between different systems is always a challenge, and especially so when you're dealing with radically different geometries of meaning, as with the Tree and the Wheel. In terms of the Tree of Life, I equate Ceugant with the Supernal Triad; Annwn doesn't really fit into that system well, since the Tree portrays the universe as the unfolding of a single transcendent reality, while the Druidical teaching portrays it as the balanced interaction of two basic realities. That said, keep working with the symbolism you're developing and see where it leads you!

replies.title: Re: Big Tech Stalkers

As anonymous and JMG said, the tech companies put a lot of money and effort into gathering data on folks and correlating it to try to get their attention (so as to place ads in front of them). Two fairly simple ways this might have happened, each creepier than the last:

1) If you use Google as your search engine or gmail as your email address and you are logged into your Google/Youtube account, they correlate between different services. That's why they give you lots of free space and encourage you to archive emails rather than delete them - they feed all of those into their algorithms to work out what might grab your attention.

2) I have a friend who was at a tech conference a few years back. There had been rumors (and still are) that the Facebook app on your phone might turn on your microphone in the background and listen to what you're talking about and then try to show you ads related to that. My friend met someone from Facebook who said "oh yeah, we totally can do that, we've tested it and it works, but we haven't started using it yet". Since then, he, myself, and others have had the experience of encountering ads on facebook based on something that we haven't ever searched for, but did have a normal conversation about in the proximity of a smartphone, suggesting that it has been turned on. It seems rather plausible Google/Youtube would do the same thing

replies.title: Re: Big Tech Stalkers

replies.title: Re: Big Tech Stalkers
replies.content:
You know, I don't think tech people feeding their bigotries into the system is even necessary. To adapt a useful term from the AI Singularity people, the modern advertising-driven tech economy is fundamentally an attention maximizer -- that is to say, a system that prioritizing getting as attention and engagement out of its users as possible, since the more they pay attention the more ads they see. And there are few emotions more effective at driving attention and engagement than hatred.

So, there's a decent chance that what happened is that the algorithm has noticed that OP is interested in occultism and is feeding them anti-occult recommendations precisely so that OP will see them, get angry at them, and thus spend more time on YouTube in the process.

(Actually, hmm. JMG: how similar is this to the mechanisms of demons in the traditional lore, be it obsession or otherwise? There's a fit between modern algorithms and the "2 + 2 = petunias" illogic you've described as characteristic of demons, and I could see this fitting the mechanism of the Two Contending Forces, but I'm not sure the rest of the behavior fits. comments.title: a question on a possible amulet/talisman
comments.content:
Dear JMG,

as always thanks for providing this extremely interesting and helpful public good.

Long time reader here, and have become more of a practitioner the last two years. Some astrological knowledge, a little bit of tarot, specially following your books as a guide (circles of power and hermetic qabalah), as well as a couple of synergistic reading. Have been practising the LBRP+QC+MP routine daily for close to 7 months, with some silent gratitude prayers, frankenincense and a small (moveable) temple to thoth/hermes trismagistus.
I am dimensioning my experiences to set up my question:
I will be visiting my motherlands (southamerica) in the next few months, and I will be spending some much needed detox time away from the craziness of the last few years (I am currently living in a city in northern europe). I will be going to very remote/rural areas, going the extra mile to spend a week or so completely away from the "modern world". Specifically I will be spending a few days in a protected area (as this is related to my work and research) which is inaccessible by land.. extremly pristine towards the amazon forest, which of-course means it is possibly packed with all sorts nature spirits.

first: Should I continue my rituals when there? as I banish using the golden dawn instructions, which effectively means earth, I would not want to displease the nature spirits who might already be displeased with my presence.

second: I work on conservation/protected areas and I have felt extremely lucky to be able to do so, but also recognize a lot of coincidences that have happened for me to arrive to these path, which for me materialized hand in hand with my magical proclivities. As hybrid anglo-southamerican mix (both cultural and blood) i would like to somehow see if I can make contact with a nature spirit during my stay... as a druid, is there any advice? All in all I would like to make sure I keep my work focused on the realities of conservation and not the abstractions and careerism that surrounds me, and see this visit as an opportunity to do so from a spiritual/magical side.

third: In line of the previous question. I know exactly what dates I will be visiting, and I have some instruction theoretical instruction on how to create talisman/sigil (stellar magic in particular). Would it be ideal to try to take something with me an pick an appropriate astrological time/benefactor and do a ritual during my week stay in the forest? If so, and if deciding for a planet, who would be ideal? (I have good mercury,moon,jupiter placements on my natal, but not so much venus/sun.. which somehow feel more appropriate). This last question is exploratory, I am open to any recommendation (a small crystal and a prayer for example, anything really).

Thanks in advance!! and again thank you because your work has certainly been a positive influence in me

replies.title: Re: a question on a possible amulet/talisman
replies.content:
1) You should certainly keep up your practices, but you might want to do the work indoors so as not to inconvenience the local nature spirits.

2) Start by doing divination to see whether this is a good idea. If it is, once you arrive, pray to the nature spirits, explain to them who you are and what you want to do, and see if you get a response. It's entirely up to them, you know.

3) That's not a question I can answer, since it depends on the fine details of when you'll be there and what your natal chart looks like. If you do, consider doing it for the benefit of the forest rather than for your own benefit -- the nature spirits might be pleased by that. Always, though, do divination first!

comments.title: Question and information on Travis Scott concert disaster
comments.content:
Hi JMG, this post is partly a question and partly a collection of information on the Travis Scott concert disaster. I'm interested to hear your thoughts on the occult dimensions of this incident based on information that's come out.

A basic summary for readers: rapper Travis Scott performed on November 5th at a music festival in Houston, Texas called Astroworld. Organization at the concert was very poor, people were allowed to crowd into the area far in excess of its capacity and chaos ensued. People were trampled and unable to breathe and 10 have died so far, including a 9-year-old boy. Promotional materials and graphics associated with the show showcase some disturbing occult imagery. People at the show have recounted a frightening atmosphere with many describing a sense of evil in the air. Someone on 4chan noted that the concert date was 666 months and 6 days from the founding of the Church of Satan. Travis Scott kept performing even as ambulances were pushing through the audience and people's motionless bodies were being crowd-surfed to paramedics. Multiple people have mentioned the disturbing behavior of concert staff who almost seemed to not hear them when they begged them to stop the show because people were dying.

The concert's tag line is "see you on the other side" and this motif is portrayed in its posters and graphics as well as on the shirt Scott was wearing during the performance, which portrays a human figure passing through a portal(?) and changing color from blue to orange. Another interesting detail: concertgoers have mentioned that for hours before the show started, the sound system was playing a continuous, low-frequency drone that people have described as "demonic." This isn't typical for hip-hop or other popular music genres and people have mentioned that it felt strange and out of place. There's been some conversation about this sound being a natural fear-stimulating frequency.

replies.title: Re: Question and information on Travis Scott concert disaster
replies.content:
As I was noting in the comments of the main blog a few weeks back, Mesoamerica is an odd duck. I very much suspect that the core rationale for human sacrifice in those parts is best described by analogy: men are to gods as maize is to men (that is to say, in the Mesoamerican view gods raise and reap men for their sustenance), and this would imply something fundamentally amoral (Nature red in tooth and claw) rather than immoral. (A lot of the region's old culture may be downstream of this: when your gods are fundamentally predators, how do you live, especially if you want to keep living?) How that analogy interacts with Teotihuacan is an interesting question (IIRC that city-state alone shows no archaeological evidence of a sacrifice tradition, and AIUI even in Aztec times Quetzalcoatl/the Feathered Serpent was notable as the one Aztec deity who didn't want sacrifices), but unfortunately to the best of my knowledge there is no evidence that the Teotihuacani ever developed writing and given that it's a question unlikely to be answered.

That said, you're not wrong, it's just that you might be painting with an overbroad brush - I'm not sure about the Maya, even during the later days of their civilization, but the *Aztecs* almost certainly turned to black magic to try to preserve their civilization and paid the price. (The Legend of the Five Suns sounds suspiciously like it describes vampirism at a cosmic scale.)

- pretentious_username

comments.title: Concept
comments.content: Scrying, astral projection, pathworking... Sorry for my conceptual sloppiness, but I’d like to know what’s the difference among them.

replies.title: Re: Concept
replies.content:
Astral projection is also known as out-of-body experience, and that sums up the most important fact about it -- you're actually outside your physical body, and can look at it from outside, etc.

Scrying is also known as remote viewing. It can be done in several ways -- some people look into the proverbial crystal ball, or some other object of that kind, while others simply use their imaginations as the medium in which images take shape. Scrying can bring glimpses of the material world or of the inner planes.

Pathworking can be one of two things. Some people say "pathworking" when they mean "guided meditation." More correctly, it's scrying aimed at that aspect of the upper astral plane where reality takes the form of certain stable centers of consciousness and meaning connected by paths, transitions, or passages. You do pathworking to learn how to move smoothly from one of these to another.

replies.title: Re: Concept
replies.content:
Apart from from looking at your body from the outside, what would be the other practical and magical uses if any of astral projection.

Thanks.

replies.title: Re: Concept
replies.content: First, if you ever had any doubts about whether you'll still be around when your body dies, that will settle them. Second, it makes certain kinds of remote viewing and the like easier and more intensive. Beyond that?  It's mostly a stunt, though some schools teach people to do it.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: We know exactly how astrology evolved, and it did take thousands of years. In Mesopotamia, right around the time that writing was first invented, astronomer-priests began tracking the movements of the heavens and writing down the details. Their religion taught them that the sun, the moon, and the planets were deities, and so it occurred to them to see if watching their movements and relative positions would reveal something about how the deities interacted with each other and humanity. That process started sometime well before 3500 BC. Within a thousand years or so they'd figured out enough to make reliable predictions about politics; by 600 BC, the first personal horoscopes were being cast; and a couple of centuries after that, the Greeks got hold of the results and carried them all over the ancient world. Astrology is still evolving in the same way; every time a new planet is discovered, astrologers go to work figuring out what its influence does by seeing how it relates to horoscopes.

comments.content: How do reading and watching films differ, in terms of their effects on the inner planes? I recently watched a somewhat disturbing film at the behest of a friend, and found that some of the more striking scenery lingered vividly in my mind, bypassing my conscious rejections of it much in the way an ad for fizzy brown sugar water might do. She loves horror films, and doesn’t practice magic so is probably unaware of what I suspect is an influence from them that goes beyond entertaining, or imparting some sort of artistic value. I was thinking about how it was probably quite unhealthy to fill one’s astral body with the violent imagery and perverse concepts which are often discussed by characters in these sort of films (even if one rejects them at the conscious level, realizing it is, at least theoretically, merely a depiction and not an endorsement), so was thinking I wouldn’t follow any more of my friend’s recommendations. But then yesterday, I found myself thoroughly enjoying my read through “The Murders in the Rue Morgue”. While I’m sure it would have been a very different experience on video, it had me feeling somewhat hypocritical and thinking I’d been a little judgemental or pearl-clutching regarding my friend’s tastes, but I still feel there could be a significant difference between the effects of the two media.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: There's a very significant difference. Reading calls up images out of your own imagination, and so strengthens your imaginative powers. (Listening to radio programs has the same effect, interestingly.) Visual media imports its own imagery, drowning out your imaginative imagery. Your mind also knows how to tell the difference between your imagination and what you see with your eyes, but it doesn't know how to tell the difference between visual media and reality -- that's why people who watch lots of TV end up believing what they see on it, no matter how absurd that is.

replies.title: Re: Geomancy

1) I'm going to suggest that you study the figures and meditate on this. You need to have a solid intuitive grasp of the emergies of the figures before you do any magic with them, and study and meditation will get that a lot more surely than asking me.

3) Yes, you trace over it with whatever you're using to write with, and "whatever" is the appropriate term.

4) Of course it is. That's why I suggest doing it!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Choose a system, any system -- it really doesn't matter which one. Choose it, commit to it, and follow the instructions exactly to the letter, changing nothing. Complete your studies -- this will usually take 5 to 10 years, depending on the system. Then you can look at other forms of magic, and begin moving toward a personal synthesis. Until you've mastered at least one system of magic, however, you don't have the background to go deep.

replies.title: Re: Choosing a system
replies.content:
1) Whether or not it resonates you is irrelevant. I mean that quite literally! At various points in your training, no matter what system you pick, it won't resonate with you; what you need to do is just keep on doing the work, and you'll gain the benefits. Nor does it matter which one to pick, because there's no such thing as a wrong system, or a right one. You can choose one by rolling dice if you like, or by picking one that has a name you like. Just pick one, start work, and keep at it until you know the whole system inside and out.

2) Here again, it's up to you. Read a bunch of books on different mythologies and see what attracts you.

3) The best aspect to begin magical training is always right now. The second best is a minute from now.

replies.title: Re: Questions
replies.content: Get a notebook and a pen. Write at the top of the first page: "What's with the calcite candle holder?" Then start writing down whatever comes into your mind. replies.title: Re: Questions
replies.content: 1) I work with Santa Muerte and St. Jesus Malverde.  They both appreciate tobacco offerings, which can include me smoking it vicariously for them.  I haven't noticed any ill effects.  In fact, I've noticed a rather pleasant feeling afterward, with a kind of heightened sense of others emotions and my ability to feel a spiritual presence around me.

replies.title: Re: Questions
replies.content: Calcite is a form of Calcium carbonate. The cell salt Calcarea carbonica was originally made of crushed oyster shells. According to Wikipedia "Calcite is a common constituent of sedimentary rocks, limestone in particular, much of which is formed from the shells of dead marine organisms. Approximately 10% of sedimentary rock is limestone". That might point towards a sea connection.

replies.title: Re: Questions
replies.content:
In my experience -- as an anglo east TN native who grew up around a lot of adopted native ceremony -- it's often used as a "grounding" force, and considered to have masculine energy (cornmeal on the other hand having feminine energy). We'd often start dance ceremonies by making a series of "prayer ties", made by wrapping several pinches of tobacco in red cloth, tying them sequentially to a long string, and infusing them with prayers. These would usually be hung in the dance arbor at the individual dancer's resting place until the end of the dance, at which we would burn them to release the prayers to the spirits.

That may vary depending on which tradition you're following though.

comments.title: Mabigonion
comments.content:
Hi JMG

The Mabigonion is incredibly complex. So much more so then really any other mythology I have encountered. But then it isn't really mythology either is it? It is something else. Is the earth not evolving and our species not evolving and becoming more complex?

We (westerners at least) have none of these magics, no real belief or conception of the realms these characters live in and travel through. What is going in here? These tales,if that is what you call them, are from prehistory? Why have we fallen so far into idiocy?
Shaking my head.

replies.title: Re: Mabigonion
replies.content:
Hmm. Have you read many original works of mythology and legend -- original as distinct from modern dumbed-down retellings? If not, you're in for a treat.

But I can answer your question very easily. No, we are not evolving and becoming more complex as a species. Progress is a myth. Each of us individually is evolving from life to life, but when we finish the lessons of this human level we move on, and other, younger souls take our place. We are not more complex than the people who wrote the Mabinogion; we are facing the same stage of evolution they were, and after we've gone onto other things, other souls will be facing that same stage in turn.

There's also an important difference between our culture and theirs. Our culture, modern Western industrial culture, had for its task figuring out what could be done with physical matter and energy. We've pursued that path about as far as it goes, and in the process we've neglected the development of ourselves. The stories of the Mabinogion are descended from ancient cultures that focused on individual development instead; they had much simpler technology and much richer spirituality than we do.

And when you speak of "we westerners," er, remember that not all Westerners have abandoned magic and the experience of the inner realms... ;-)
